name: Peñarroya Castle Route
name_original: Presa del Embalse de Peñarroya — circular desde Argamasilla de Alba
description: This moderate 18.3-mile touring cycling route from Argamasilla de Alba circles the Peñarroya Reservoir, passing the historic Peñarroya Castl
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Argamasilla De Alba are situated in the heart of La Mancha, characterized by a vast, generally flat to gently rolling plain. The region is influenced by the Alto Guadiana river basin and features the unique aquatic landscape of the Lagunas de Ruidera Natural Park, with its stepped lakes and waterfalls. The Peñarroya Reservoir also provides a notable water feature, often overlooked by the historic Peñarroya Castle. This varied terrain offers a mix of open landscapes and natural water bodies for cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Argamasilla De Alba The most popular touring cycling route is Molinos de Alcázar de San Juan y Campo de Criptana — circular desde Tomelloso, a 46.9 miles (75.5 km) trail that takes 5 hours 12 minutes to complete, passing by historic windmills. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Peñarroya Castle Route, a moderate 18.3 miles (29.5 km) path. This route offers views of the Peñarroya Reservoir and the historic castle. Local touring cyclists also love the Parque Natural Lagunas de Rudeira — circular desde Tomelloso, a 38.6 miles (62.2 km) trail leading through the Lagunas de Ruidera Natural Park, often completed in about 4 hours 44 minutes. Touring cycling around Argamasilla De Alba is defined by the open plains of La Mancha, the unique Lagunas de Ruidera, and historical landmarks like Peñarroya Castle. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy greenways to more challenging long-distance routes. The routes in Argamasilla De Alba are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from around 50 reviews. More than 600 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Argamasilla De Alba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Argamasilla De Alba?
A: There are over 60 touring cycling routes around Argamasilla De Alba, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. These routes explore the diverse landscapes of La Mancha and the Alto Guadiana region.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in this region?
A: The region is characterized by a vast, generally flat to gently rolling plain, making it suitable for touring cyclists of various fitness levels. While many routes are on paved surfaces, some may include unpaved segments, especially those venturing into natural areas like the Lagunas de Ruidera Natural Park.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as circular paths, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057464109/molinos-de-alcazar-de-san-juan-y-campo-de-criptana-circular-desde-tomelloso" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molinos de Alcázar de San Juan y Campo de Criptana — circular desde Tomelloso&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular route that takes you past historic windmills. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057518244/parque-natural-lagunas-de-rudeira-circular-desde-tomelloso" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural Lagunas de Rudeira — circular desde Tomelloso&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the natural park.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The routes offer a blend of natural beauty and historical sites. You can explore the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5573792" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Rey&lt;/a&gt; with its turquoise waters, or witness the cascading &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3844258" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Batanas Lagoon Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5884091" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Hundimiento Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; within the Lagunas de Ruidera Natural Park. The historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057436658/penarroya-castle-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñarroya Castle Route&lt;/a&gt; offers views of the castle and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2723495" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñarroya Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are 7 easy touring cycling routes around Argamasilla De Alba. The region's generally flat terrain, including the local 2.31 km Argamasilla de Alba Greenway, provides accessible options for those seeking a more relaxed ride or cycling with family. While specific family-friendly routes aren't detailed in the data, the presence of greenways and easy routes suggests suitable options.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Argamasilla De Alba?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, the Mediterranean climate of La Mancha generally offers pleasant cycling conditions in spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder. Summers can be hot, and winters can be cool, so checking local weather forecasts is always recommended.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by the famous windmills of La Mancha?
A: Absolutely! The region is deeply connected to Don Quijote, and you can cycle past iconic windmills. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057464109/molinos-de-alcazar-de-san-juan-y-campo-de-criptana-circular-desde-tomelloso" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molinos de Alcázar de San Juan y Campo de Criptana — circular desde Tomelloso&lt;/a&gt; route is specifically designed to take you past these historic structures.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Argamasilla De Alba?
A: The touring cycling routes in Argamasilla De Alba are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from around 50 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from open plains to the unique aquatic landscapes of the Lagunas de Ruidera, and the historical landmarks encountered along the way.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the Lagunas de Ruidera Natural Park?
A: Yes, the Lagunas de Ruidera Natural Park is a significant highlight for touring cyclists. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057518244/parque-natural-lagunas-de-rudeira-circular-desde-tomelloso" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural Lagunas de Rudeira — circular desde Tomelloso&lt;/a&gt; is a dedicated route that leads through this spectacular landscape of stepped lakes and waterfalls.

Q: How long do the touring cycling routes typically take to complete?
A: Route durations vary significantly based on distance and difficulty. For instance, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057436658/penarroya-castle-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñarroya Castle Route&lt;/a&gt; (29.5 km) takes approximately 2 hours and 23 minutes, while more challenging routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057464109/molinos-de-alcazar-de-san-juan-y-campo-de-criptana-circular-desde-tomelloso" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molinos de Alcázar de San Juan y Campo de Criptana — circular desde Tomelloso&lt;/a&gt; (75.5 km) can take over 5 hours.

Q: Are there any routes that offer significant elevation gain for a more challenging ride?
A: While the region is generally flat, some routes do offer more significant elevation changes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052946665/ruidera-circular-from-castillo-de-penarroya" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruidera — circular from Castillo de Peñarroya&lt;/a&gt; route involves nearly 480 meters of elevation gain, providing a more demanding experience for cyclists seeking a challenge.

Q: What historical sites can I visit while cycling in the area?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, the region is rich in history. You can cycle to the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057436658/penarroya-castle-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñarroya Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which houses the Sanctuary of Nuestra Señora de Peñarroya. The area's connection to Miguel de Cervantes' "Don Quijote" also adds a literary historical dimension to your ride.
tips: This town in the province of Ciudad Real is not only known for its Guadiana River and the Lagunas de Ruidera Natural Park, but also because this village is the famous place that Cervantes did not want to mention in his quintessential work, Don Quixote de La Mancha.
Road that passes along the Canal of the Left Bank of the Peñarroya Reservoir. Very quiet, without traffic, perfect for cycling.

You are at the back of the Peñarroya reservoir dam, for hydroelectric and supply use located in the municipal area of the town of Argamasilla de Alba, next to the Peñarroya Castle which you can see on the left. The reservoir was inaugurated in 1959, although the first projects date back to 1917.

faqs.question: Where does the Presa del Embalse de Peñarroya — circular desde Argamasilla de Alba route start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route typically starts from Argamasilla de Alba. While specific parking details aren't provided, as a starting point for a circular route, you'll generally find parking options within the town itself, especially near the main access points to the rural roads leading out to the reservoir.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied, offering a diverse cycling experience. You'll encounter a mix of paved roads, including sections of the CM-3105 near Peñarroya Dam and Peñarroya Castle. However, many parts of the route also involve dirt tracks, rural roads, and forest tracks. Be aware that some asphalted sections, like those maintained by the Guadiana Hydrographic Confederation, might be in poor condition. After heavy rain, certain unpaved sections can become muddy and more challenging.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers several notable sights. A major highlight is the historic Peñarroya Dam and Peñarroya Castle, an ancient Muslim fort offering panoramic views of the reservoir. You'll also cycle along the Canal Road, enjoying the unique scenery of the Natural Park of Lagunas de Ruidera, with its clear waters and diverse riparian vegetation.

faqs.question: Is this touring cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate. While some sections might be straightforward, the varied terrain, including dirt and rural tracks, and the overall distance of nearly 30 km, suggest it's best suited for cyclists with some experience. Beginners might find it challenging, especially if conditions are muddy after rain. For a more relaxed experience, consider shorter sections or ensure you're comfortable with mixed terrain.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, temperatures are milder and more pleasant for cycling. Summer can be very hot in La Mancha, making longer rides uncomfortable, while intense rain in other seasons can make dirt tracks muddy and difficult to navigate.

faqs.question: Is the Presa del Embalse de Peñarroya — circular desde Argamasilla de Alba route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the area is generally open for outdoor activities, specific rules for dogs on this particular cycling route are not detailed. It's advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near the reservoir, and to ensure they are comfortable with the varied terrain and distance. Always carry water for your dog and clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are mentioned for cycling this route. The area, including the Natural Park of Lagunas de Ruidera, is generally accessible to the public for recreational activities. However, always respect local regulations and private property signs.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route partially overlaps with other established trails. Approximately 53% of the route follows sections of the Sendero Priorato de San Juan, and about 51% coincides with parts of the Camino Natural del Guadiana. This means you might encounter signage or features related to these longer trails during your ride.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants along the route or in Argamasilla de Alba?
faqs.answer: While the route itself primarily passes through natural and rural areas, Argamasilla de Alba, as the starting and ending point, offers various amenities. You can find cafes, restaurants, and other services in the town to refuel before or after your ride.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see along the reservoir?
faqs.answer: The area around the Peñarroya Reservoir, being part of the Natural Park of Lagunas de Ruidera, is rich in biodiversity. You might spot diverse aquatic and riverside fauna, including various bird species like the golden oriole and kingfisher. The riparian vegetation, such as poplar groves, also supports a variety of smaller creatures.

faqs.question: Is it possible to rent bikes in Argamasilla de Alba for this route?
faqs.answer: While not explicitly stated for this specific route, Argamasilla de Alba is a town that serves as a gateway to outdoor activities. It's advisable to check with local tourism offices or search online for bike rental services in or near Argamasilla de Alba if you need to rent a touring bicycle for this route.
